title: Architecture as Inductive Bias & Interpretability
summary: The architecture defines the functional form of q_θ, determining which parameters exist and how they interact. This structure constrains the space of possible solutions and provides the conceptual framework through which humans can understand what the model has learned.
key_insights: Supervised learning minimizes D_KL(p || q_θ) — the statistical objective doesn't care about architecture
Architecture defines which function family q_θ belongs to: Q_transformer, Q_CNN, Q_linear, etc.
Architecture makes the learned solution interpretable: embeddings → geometry, attention → patterns, MLP → detectors
Architecture = inductive bias = constraints that shape what kind of q_θ can be learned
Without architecture, q_θ = giant lookup table — uninterpretable. With architecture, q_θ = structured latent space

mathematical_core.architecture_role: Defines the set Q such that q_θ ∈ Q_architecture
mathematical_core.function_families: Q_transformer ⊂ Q_all, Q_CNN ⊂ Q_all, Q_linear ⊂ Q_all — each a different subset of all possible functions

derivations.title: KL divergence as the unifying training objective

steps.name: Forward KL
steps.math: D_KL(p || q_θ) = Σ_x p(x) log(p(x)/q_θ(x)) = -H(p) - Σ_x p(x) log q_θ(x)

steps.name: Training equivalence
steps.math: Since H(p) is constant w.r.t. θ: argmin_θ D_KL(p || q_θ) = argmin_θ -Σ_x p(x) log q_θ(x) = argmin_θ cross-entropy loss

steps.name: Architecture independence
steps.math: This objective is valid for ANY q_θ — linear, CNN, transformer. The architecture only constrains WHICH q_θ functions are reachable, not the objective itself.

derivations.consequence: Cross-entropy loss IS KL minimization. The architecture determines the search space, the loss determines the direction.

derivations.title: Inductive bias as function space restriction
derivations.math: Universal approximation: a sufficiently large MLP can approximate any function. But transformers approximate SOME functions much more efficiently (fewer parameters, faster convergence) because their structure matches the data's structure. The 'bias' is toward functions that decompose into attention (relational) + FFN (transformational) + residual (refinement) patterns.
derivations.consequence: Architecture choice = betting that your data has the structure your architecture is biased toward. Transformers bet on: sequential data with long-range dependencies and position-dependent relationships.

enables: All architecture topics — explains WHY architecture matters

application_relevance: When adapting an architecture, you are choosing which aspects of the solution will be interpretable and which relationships the model can discover. This is the theoretical foundation for architectural innovation.
